2604.15794.title: Self-Distillation as a Performance Recovery Mechanism for LLMs: Counteracting Compression and Catastrophic Forgetting
2604.15794.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success, underpinning diverse AI applications. However, they often suffer from performance degradation due to factors such as catastrophic forgetting during Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), quantization, and pruning. In this work, we introduce a performance recovery framework based on Self-Distillation Fine-Tuning (SDFT) that effectively restores model capabilities. Complementing this practical contribution, we provide a rigorous theoretical explanation for the underlying recovery mechanism. We posit that an LLM's generative capability fundamentally relies on the high-dimensional manifold constructed by its hidden layers. To investigate this, we employ Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA) to quantify the alignment between student and teacher activation trajectories, leveraging its invariance to orthogonal transformations and scaling. Our experiments demonstrate a strong correlation between performance recovery and manifold alignment, substantiating the claim that self-distillation effectively aligns the student's high-dimensional manifold with the optimal structure represented by the teacher. This study bridges the gap between practical recovery frameworks and geometric representation theory, offering new insights into the internal mechanisms of self-distillation.
2604.15794.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已取得顯著的成功，支撐著各種人工智慧應用。<lb/>然而，它們經常因為如在監督微調（SFT）過程中的災難性遺忘、量化和剪枝等因素而導致性能下降。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們介紹了一種基於自我蒸餾微調（SDFT）的性能恢復框架，能有效恢復模型的能力。<lb/>除了這一實用貢獻外，我們還提供了對基礎恢復機制的嚴謹理論解釋。<lb/>我們假設，LLM的生成能力根本上依賴於其隱藏層構建的高維流形。<lb/>為了探討這一點，我們採用中心核對齊（CKA）來量化學生和教師激活軌跡之間的對齊，利用其對正交變換和縮放的不變性。<lb/>我們的實驗顯示性能恢復與流形對齊之間存在強相關性，證實了自我蒸餾有效地將學生的高維流形與教師所代表的最佳結構對齊的說法。<lb/>本研究彌合了實用恢復框架與幾何表示理論之間的鴻溝，為自我蒸餾的內部機制提供了新的見解。
2604.15794.author: Chi Liu et.al.
2604.15794.authors: Chi Liu, Xin Chen, Xu Zhou, Fangbo Tu, Srinivasan Manoharan
